inline_comments.message:
it is a new healthcheck test
as you indicated the tag "core" it will be automatically added in the core results => https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/daily/onap_daily_pod4_master/2021-12/20_02-13/xtesting-healthcheck/core/core/report.html

no problem to test the mariadb connectivity

is it not partially covered by the onap-k8s test that checks the status of the kubernetes cluster...and some pods can only start if the mariadb is available (liveness/readiness pods will fail if not), if so, this test will be a confirmation or do you have in mind some cases where the pods successfully started without a mariadb pod connectivity?

Yes mariadb connectivity is partially covered by the onap-k8s test.

Below are the steps that we are proposing

1. We are checking if the MariaDB pod is in running state by logging into the pod and printing custom message 

2. 'SO' has 3 DB(catalogdb,requestdb,camundabpmn) hosted in MariaDB, so to check the connectivity between 'SO-MariaDB' we are logging into 'MariaDB-SO' databases and validate connectivity of all three SO-databases.
